connection_context: Dogger and Hornsea are the strongest current fit for east-coast export routes, especially BritNed, with France-facing hubs as a secondary southbound option.
East Anglia is the cleanest south-east offshore export candidate, with BritNed first and France-facing hubs following similar corridor families.
Humber sits between the direct east-coast export candidates and the broader North Sea transfer family; it is useful but still less curated than East Anglia or Hornsea.
North Wales is primarily a western-flow counterexample in the current model and should not be over-read into south-east export routes.
East-coast Scotland can plausibly reach BritNed first, with France-facing hubs depending on additional southbound transfer.
North-east Scotland offshore output still depends on north-to-south internal transfer before southern export hubs are meaningfully reachable.
Shetland remains structurally upstream-constrained because island-to-mainland transfer must clear before any interconnector route matters.
